exemplar.title: Personal Project - Baking App - Dough Flow

detailedExemplarData.title: Personal Project - Baking App - Dough Flow

strengths: Clear selection of Self-Management skills for achieving the learning goal and Communication skills for the product
Well-described affective skills, showing how stress-management techniques supported your focus
Organizational strategies are distinct and show you have structured your work effectively
Thorough outline of how you plan to implement communication through media
weaknesses: Descriptions of ATL skill application lack concrete, time-stamped examples tied to deliverables
Skills are not yet explicitly aligned to specific success criteria to show direct impact
Communication modules are listed without linking each skill to design choices or user testing outcomes
Missing user feedback or testing data to evidence how communication strategies improved the app
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your application of ATL skills is thoughtfully chosen and you provide clear overviews of self-management, affective, organizational, and communication strategies. To strengthen this section, include precise examplesâsuch as dates when you used coping strategies to meet a milestoneâand explicitly map each ATL skill to one or more success criteria. Incorporating snippets of user feedback or test results will demonstrate how your communication approaches directly enhanced the productâs quality.

strengths: Strong rationale linking personal interest in baking and design to the learning goal
Clear consultation plan with design teacher and peers, showing proactive feedback integration
Effective connection of prior Figma experience to the appâs design process
weaknesses: Inclusion of an unrelated âTravelâ branch dilutes focus on the baking app
Learning goal is broad and lacks measurable targets for prototype completion or user testing
Research and survey plan is missing clear sequencing and timeframes
Success criteria table does not specify how each criterion will be measured
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your planning demonstrates a solid foundation: you effectively tie your passion for baking and design into a clear learning goal, and you show foresight by engaging mentors and leveraging past Figma experience. However, the plan would benefit from tighter focus and rigor. Removing unrelated branches (such as âTravelâ) will keep your planning diagram concise. Defining specific, measurable targetsâlike the number of prototype screens or user responsesâand adding a timeline for each research and development task will make your success criteria and overall plan more actionable and feasible.

strengths: Balanced evaluation of self-management, outlining both virtues and limitations
Actionable âNextâ steps that show commitment to improving self-management
Insightful analysis of communication strengths and weaknesses tied to design elements
Comprehensive reflection on learning impact, linking skill development to the IB learner profile
weaknesses: Lacks detailed SMART goals in next steps to address tendencies toward over-planning
Does not propose concrete preventative strategies or tools for future challenges
Reflection could more explicitly tie benefits back to specific success criteria and user survey results
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflective analysis is thorough and shows genuine critical thinkingâparticularly in assessing self-management and communication skills. You offer clear next steps and connect your growth to the IB learner profile. To deepen your reflection, frame your goals as SMART (Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound) and outline concrete tools (e.g., a task-tracking app or peer check-ins) to prevent recurring issues. Additionally, explicitly reference success criteria or user survey findings when discussing communication benefits to demonstrate the full impact of your work.

annotations.comment: Strong rationale linking your personal interest in baking and design to the learning goal. Consider enriching this with specific user-needs examples you uncovered in early tests.
Your planning diagram includes a âTravelâ branch thatâs unrelated to the baking app. Focusing all planning elements directly on app development will enhance relevance and clarity.
Your evaluation of self-management balances virtues and limitations effectively, showing critical reflection. You might enhance readability by summarizing key points in bullets.
Your âNextâ steps for improving self-management are actionable. To refine further, set SMART goals for your planning process to address tendencies to over-plan.
The transition to product-focused ATL skills needs concrete evidence. Specify exact moments or deliverables where Communication skills directly enhanced your app quality.

Your learning goal is clearly stated, but itâs broad and lacks measurable targets. Define specific metrics for success (e.g., completing X prototype screens or gathering Y user responses) to strengthen your criteria.
The product goal identifies functionality and aesthetics well but would benefit from detailed success criteria that are measurable and tied to resources and timelines.
You outline relevant research and surveys but omit sequencing and timeframes. Adding a clear timeline for each task will improve feasibility and planning rigor.
Good explanation of consulting your design teacher and peers. Strengthen this by specifying how you will integrate and track their feedback in your development process.
Your success criteria table is thorough but could be clearer on how you will measure each item. For instance, specify survey questions or usability metrics tied to each criterion.
You effectively connect prior Figma experience to your project. Enhance this by detailing which specific features or workflows you will leverage for your appâs design.
You clearly select Self-Management for the learning goal and Communication for the product. Next, align each chosen ATL skill to specific success criteria to demonstrate direct impact.
Your overview of Self-Management modules is comprehensive but lacks concrete examples. Add specific instancesâdates or tasksâwhere coping methods or motivation strategies made a difference.
Affective skills are well described. To strengthen, cite precise moments (e.g., tight deadlines) when stress-management techniques improved your focus or outcomes.
Your organizational strategies are clear. Consider adding evidence of improved productivity, such as completed milestones or reduced task overruns after scheduling improvements.
You list Communication skill modules clearly; strengthen by linking each skill (e.g., non-verbal cues) to specific actions or design choices in your app.
Your outline of implementing communication through media is thorough. To enhance, include user feedback or testing data that demonstrates the impact of these choices.
Insightful evaluation of communication limitations. To deepen, propose alternative strategies or tools for creating more personal interaction within the app.
Well-articulated virtues of communication tied to design elements. Strengthen by explicitly linking each benefit back to your success criteria and user survey results.
Your reflection on the learning impact is comprehensive. Consider distilling key takeaways into succinct points to clearly show how your knowledge extended in app development.
Strong connection between skill development and broader IB learner profile growth. Further strengthen by citing examples of how these skills improved subsequent tasks.
You insightfully describe challenges and solutions. To improve, outline concrete preventative strategies or tools you will use in future projects to avoid similar issues.
